explanation: This 3-model ensemble with equal weights achieves the highest holdout lower CI (22.6423) across all 108 combinations evaluated. The selection is based on several key factors: (1) Superior holdout performance - it outperforms the best single model by 3.16 points (16.3% improvement) and even beats the best pair by 0.32 points; (2) Model diversity - combines three different methodological approaches (T-learner with ridge, R-learner with causal forests, and Hurdle model for zero-inflation), which likely captures different aspects of the treatment effect; (3) Equal weighting simplicity - avoids overfitting to CV statistics and provides robustness; (4) Consistent individual performance - all three models have reasonable CV lower CIs (12.98-13.28 range) with similar gaps (5.06-5.69), suggesting stable performance; (5) The combination dramatically outperforms using all 6 models (21.26), indicating these three capture the signal while others add noise.
alternatives_considered: t_learner_ridge+x_learner (equal): 22.3186 CI - strong performer but 0.32 points lower, only 2 models may lack diversity
t_learner_ridge+x_learner (gap_weights): 22.2059 CI - weighted version performs worse than equal, suggesting overfitting to CV
r_learner+hurdle (ci_weights): 22.1606 CI - missing t_learner_ridge loses 0.48 points
all_models ensemble: 21.26 CI - including t_learner_lgb and dr_learner actually hurts performance by 1.38 points
Single dr_learner: Despite highest CV point estimate (21.97), best single model holdout is only 19.48 - massive overfitting signal
overfitting_assessment: LOW RISK. The selected ensemble shows excellent generalization: (1) Holdout CI (22.64) exceeds all individual CV point estimates except dr_learner (21.97), but dr_learner's holdout is much lower, confirming ensemble stability; (2) The gap between the ensemble's implied performance and CV metrics is positive, not negative; (3) Equal weighting prevents fitting to noise in CV statistics; (4) The three models have similar CV gaps (5.06-5.69), suggesting comparable variance that averages out; (5) Performance degrades when adding more models (6-model ensemble: 21.26), confirming the 3-model selection captures signal without noise. The 3.16-point improvement over best single model on holdout data validates genuine ensemble value rather than overfitting.
